frozen_components: base LLM parameters (only LoRA adapters trained)
architecture.summary: CLaRa is a unified retrieval-augmented generation framework that compresses documents into shared continuous vector representations (memory tokens) and jointly optimizes retrieval and generation end-to-end. Information flows: (1) documents are compressed offline via a frozen compressor into dense embeddings, (2) queries are encoded by a trainable query reasoner into the same embedding space, (3) top-k documents are selected via differentiable Straight-Through estimation, (4) selected compressed representations are concatenated with the query and fed to a generator LoRA that produces answers, (5) gradients from the generator's next-token prediction loss flow back through the top-k selector to update both the query reasoner and the retriever implicitly. The system operates in two stages: SCP (Salient Compressor Pretraining) synthesizes QA and paraphrase supervision to train the compressor, then CLaRa performs end-to-end joint training using only the language modeling loss without explicit relevance labels.
trained_components: query reasoner LoRA (during CLaRa end-to-end training)

biblio.title: CLaRa: Bridging Retrieval and Generation with Continuous Latent Reasoning

claims.statement: CLaRa-Mistral-7B with 16× compression surpasses text-based DRO-Mistral-7B, improving F1 from 51.01% to 51.41% on NQ and from 43.65% to 47.18% on 2Wiki
Compared to PISCO (best soft compression baseline), SCP-Mistral-7B at 4× compression achieves average gains of 1.13% across four benchmarks (NQ, HotpotQA, MuSiQue, 2WikiMultihopQA)
CLaRa-Mistral-7B (pretraining-initialized, 4× compression) achieves Recall@5 of 96.21% on HotpotQA, exceeding supervised baseline BGE-Reranker (85.93%) by +10.28%

SCP pretraining with all three supervision types (SimpleQA + ComplexQA + Paraphrase) outperforms no-pretraining baseline by +7.14% average F1 on Oracle setting (32× compression) across four benchmarks on Mistral-7B
At 128× extreme compression, SCP-Mistral-7B outperforms XRAG baseline by +15.37% average CEM across four benchmarks
CLaRa achieves retrieval and generation performance on par with or surpassing fully supervised models despite relying solely on weak generation supervision (no annotated relevance labels)

Well-trained soft compression can retain essential reasoning information while substantially reducing input length, exceeding the text-based baseline using uncompressed documents with average gains of 2.36% on Mistral-7B and 6.36% on Phi-4-mini
Performance declines at extreme compression (beyond 32× in Oracle setting), though the drop remains moderate under Normal conditions
